name: Nid-Hca Madison
description_short: HUD-approved housing counseling agency providing free guidance on homeownership, financial stability, and affordable housing solutions through certified counselors.
description_long:
NID Housing Counseling Agency is a national HUD-approved intermediary organization that operates as a network of advocacy groups, housing counselors, real estate professionals, community organizations, and faith-based groups. The agency was established to empower individuals and communities through comprehensive housing solutions with a focus on fostering sustainable homeownership and building resilient neighborhoods across the nation.

NID offers a range of free services including one-on-one counseling with HUD-certified counselors, homebuyer education workshops, financial stability guidance, and down payment assistance program connections. The organization also provides housing counselor training and certification courses, hosts webinars and training events for housing professionals and community leaders, and collaborates with educational institutions (HBCUs and MSIs) on affordable housing initiatives. All services are provided at no cost to consumers, with the agency committing to respond to service requests within 48 hours.

The company distinguishes itself through its multi-sector network approach, combining nonprofit advocacy with real estate professionals and community partners. NID explicitly demonstrates commitment to eliminating housing disparities and improving access to quality housing and mortgage products. The organization remained operational during the recent U.S. government shutdown, highlighting organizational resilience. Case studies on their website show concrete outcomes, including clients receiving $80,000 in down payment assistance and first-time homebuyers closing on properties within structured timelines.

As a legitimate HUD-approved counseling agency, NID appears to deliver genuine free services without hidden fees or sales pressure. The organization's dual focus on consumer counseling and professional training suggests institutional depth. However, consumers should note that actual service availability may vary by location, and the website emphasizes connecting clients to "the nearest branch," suggesting geographic limitations in coverage.
pros: HUD-approved and certified, meeting federal standards for housing counseling
Completely free services with no hidden fees or charges
HUD-certified counselors available for one-on-one guidance
Documented success connecting clients to down payment assistance programs ($80,000 cited in case studies)
Rapid response commitment (48-hour maximum contact time)
Comprehensive approach including workshops, webinars, and training for professionals
Network-based model connecting clients across advocacy, real estate, and community organizations
cons: Geographic coverage appears limited with emphasis on location-based 'nearest branch' referrals
Website lacks detailed service availability by state or region
Limited transparency on current counselor availability and wait times for appointments
No explicit information about online vs. in-person counseling options
Website contains minimal detail on specific program eligibility requirements or application timelines
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking free education and down payment assistance navigation
Individuals working toward financial stability and long-term homeownership goals
Low-to-moderate income households in underserved communities with housing disparities
Housing professionals and community leaders seeking training and best practices knowledge
diagnosis: NID Housing Counseling Agency is best for first-time homebuyers and individuals seeking free, legitimate HUD-certified counseling on homeownership, down payment assistance, and financial stability. The main caveat is that actual service availability depends on geographic location and proximity to active branch locations, so consumers should verify local service before relying on the organization.
services: Down payment assistance program connections and navigation
Housing counselor certification and training courses
Webinars and training sessions for housing professionals
Community workshops on home purchase considerations
Affordable housing solutions for HBCU and MSI students
Advocacy and education on housing disparities
Resources on mortgage products and housing access
Secure existing client portal for document and case management
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 107 Brookridge Dr Ste C, Madison, AL 35758
